channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Customs releases N7.61bn to PFAs for prompt payment to 4,237 retirees
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) has released N7.61 billion to eight Pension Fund Administrators (PFAs) for payment to 4,237 retirees, as the Comptroller-General of Customs, Adewale Adeniyi, reaffirmed the service’s commitment to improving the welfare of its retired personnel. The post Customs rele

item.title: Tinubu commissions reconstructed Arab/Gbazango Road in Kubwa
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has commissioned the reconstructed Arab/Gbazango Road in Kubwa, Bwari Area Council of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), saying the project validates his administration’s Renewed Hope Agenda and its commitment to delivering visible infrastructure across the capital and the na

item.title: Oyo abduction: Igbo group backs Makinde’s call for independent probe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Coalition of South East Youth Leaders, (COSEYL), a conglomeration of Igbo youth groups in the South East, has supported Governor Seyi Makinde’s call for an independent international investigation into the abduction of pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State and the circu

item.title: Court dismisses suit by Bayelsa traditional ruler challenging Shell’s divestment, pollution
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Yenagoa Division of the Federal High Court on Friday, dismissed a suit filed by the Ibenananowei of Ekpetiama Kingdom, HRM King Bubaraye Dakolo Agada IV, challenging the divestment of Shell from onshore assets. MEkpetiama Kingdom in Yenagoa Local Government Area of Bayelsa State is a host commun

item.title: Moghalu mourns Amaechi’s mother, extends condolences
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Managing Director of the National Inland Waterways Authority (NIWA), Dr George Moghalu, has commiserated with the vice presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Rotimi Amaechi, over the death of his mother, Dame Ezinne Mary Oduah Amaechi. The post Moghalu mourns Amaechi

item.title: Targets of latest US strikes on Iran signal strategic shift
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US has broadened its military campaign in Iran, says Al Jazeera’s Mike Hanna.

item.title: Kogi announces rescue of kidnapped victims in Dekina
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Kogi State government has announced the rescue of the four persons kidnapped during the recent attack on an unauthorised examination centre in Olowa, Dekina Local Government Area of the state. The post Kogi announces rescue of kidnapped victims in Dekina appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Tinubu mourns death of Amaechi’s mother
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has expressed sorrow over the death of Dame Ezinne Mary Oduah Amaechi, mother of former Governor of Rivers State and ex-Minister of Transportation, Rotimi Amaechi, at the age of 89. The post Tinubu mourns death of Amaechi’s mother appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Police exhume corpse of alleged chicken thief, arrest 4
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Akwa Ibom State Police Command, said it has exhumed the body a 21-year-old man, identified as Emmanuel Effiong, who was lynched and buried in a shallow grave by irate mob for allegedly stealing chickens and eggs from a poultry farm in Mbak community, Uyo Local Government Area of the state. The p

item.title: Kosoko Ruling House alleges palace siege, urges police to obey court order
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: ​The Olojo Kosoko Ruling House of Lagos has accused certain individuals of using police officers to intimidate family members and enforce an unlawful siege on the Oloja of Lagos Palace, despite a subsisting court order directing parties to maintain the status quo. The post Kosoko Ruling House allege

item.title: Kaduna South launches digital transformation initiative unveils scorecard
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Kaduna South Local Government Area has launched a digital transformation initiative. The post Kaduna South launches digital transformation initiative unveils scorecard appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: What I would do if I were governor of my state
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Like adults, children have dreams, ideas, and concerns they long to express through different platforms. In this interview with Bukola Oshoko, these children imagine themselves in positions of leadership and share how they would tackle the challenges and improve the lives of citizens. Oyaniyi Nelson

item.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

item.title: SEGUN ODEGBAMI: Who wins – Spain or Argentina?
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The curtains will be drawn on the 2026 FIFA World Cup tomorrow.   The 104 football battles fought amongst 48 countries will come to a most befitting climax. The two teams at the finish line are amongst the 7 that have previously won the World Cup, maintaining a tradition that only a few co

item.title: 2026 World Cup final – Messi vs Yamal: Sorcerer and the apprentice, by Stephanie Shaakaa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: ​The 2026 FIFA World Cup final on July 19th is not merely a match; it is a funeral for the game as we knew it.  Every civilization has a moment that historians later recognize as the quiet beginning of a new age. It rarely arrives with the fanfare of revolution. More often, it wears the [&#8230

item.title: Meet Kimi K3, the newest Chinese AI model haunting Silicon Valley
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Moonshot AI’s new open model is catching up to Anthropic and OpenAI, raising questions about the future of the AI race.

item.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US command says it is conducting more strikes to degrade Iran's armed forces, as Iran reports explosions near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup final: History asking for a signature
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: This is not just a game. This is history asking for a signature. And if it happens? A second straight World Cup for Argentina — the very first in the modern era to defend the crown. The post 2026 FIFA World Cup final: History asking for a signature appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Umahi: How Mary, not Habila, destroyed a powerful senator, by Emmanuel Aziken
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: It was not merely the accident at Chappaquiddick that destroyed Senator Ted Kennedy’s lifelong ambition of becoming President of the United States. It was Mary. Mary Jo Kopechne. She was the young woman who died after Kennedy’s car plunged from a narrow bridge on Chappaquiddick Island in July 1969.

item.title: Israel: The scales are falling off, by Muyiwa Adetiba
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Theirs was a classic Father/Daughter story. She was the apple of his eyes. His Miss Nigeria if not Miss Universe. He was her knight in shining armor. The one person who could do no wrong. She sat on his knees into her teens – much longer than many puritans would deem acceptable. He was putty i

item.title: Disrupted rest linked to anxiety, pregnant women urged to prioritise sleep
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The effect of pregnancy on sleep may be far less than dreamy. As a woman’s belly grows, frequent tossing and turning, coupled with repeated trips to the bathroom, can make a restful night’s sleep feel like a luxury. But lack of sleep is more than an inconvenience. A new study by researchers at Washi

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Nigerian Navy Ship (NNS) BEECROFT has seized 757 kilograms of suspected cannabis sativa during separate operations across Lagos State. According to a report by The Nation, the illicit substances, packed in 18 bags, were intercepted in three operations carried out between April 7 and July 17 befo

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: By Hamza Arab Kabiru In every thriving economy, there exists an unseen architecture more enduring than steel, more resilient than concrete, and more valuable than the wealth it ultimately creates. It is the architecture of trust. In Nigeria’s telecommunications industry, this invisible cathedral has

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Lagos State government has re-arraigned a widow, Kafayat Oyelakin, before the state high court sitting in Ikeja for allegedly failing to stop her late husband from practising medicine despite knowing that he was not a qualified medical doctor. Oyelakin was re-arraigned before Justice Oyindamola

item.title: FG, Partners Move To Strengthen Local Drug Manufacturing For Health Security
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government and its development partners have renewed their commitment to strengthening local pharmaceutical manufacturing, describing domestic production of medicines as critical to Nigeria’s health security, economic growth and resilience against future public health emergencies.

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Kogi State Police Command has rescued a kidnap victim, neutralised a suspected kidnapper, and recovered a pump-action gun during a joint security operation in Ijumu Local Government Area. A joint security team comprising Police operatives, the Command’s Violent Crime Response Unit, officer

item.title: Rescued Oyo Teacher Says Kidnappers Blindfolded Victims, Banned Prayers In Jesus’ Name
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: One of the teachers rescued after spending 56 days in the custody of kidnappers in Oyo State, Zacchaeus Olatunde, has recounted the harrowing ordeal, revealing that the captives were permanently blindfolded, barred from praying in the name of Jesus for weeks and lived each day uncertain whether they

item.description: Whereabouts of artist imprisoned after 2021 protests had been unknown following end of his prison term in JulyLuis Manuel Otero Alcántara, a prominent Cuban dissident artist whose whereabouts remained unknown after his prison sentence ended last week, has been given permission to travel to the US, a

item.title: NRC declares war on railway vandals
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The recovered railway components, estimated to be worth about ₦200 million, were intercepted during an intelligence-led operation, a development the NRC boss described as a major breakthrough in the campaign against the destruction of railway infrastructure.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Pharmacy Council of Nigeria (PCN) has sealed 505 pharmaceutical premises in Cross River State following a four-day enforcement exercise across 10 local government areas. PCN Director of Enforcement, Dr. Suleiman Chiroma, disclosed this on Friday in Calabar, saying inspectors visited 602 premises

item.description: Deal with Chevron aims to rebuild a crude oil pipeline that runs from Iraq's oil-rich Kirkuk to Syria's Baniyas.

item.description: Aubameyang, who still had a year remaining on his contract with the Ligue 1 club, leaves after playing 92 matches and scoring 44 goals. The post Gabon forward Aubameyang leaves Marseille for Deportivo La Coruna appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG is preparing to sell about $3 billion in debt markets for accounting services firm Baker Tilly Advisory Group to refinance private credit loans, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Food producer Taylor Farms released a statement on the Cyclospora outbreak Friday, confirming that it's "voluntarily removing all iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico from the US market." Reuters reports that, according to a source, Taylor Farms told customers like Yum Brands owner Taco Bell

item.title: Microfinance Bank Employee Arraigned In Lagos Over Alleged Forgery
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A 37-year-old employee of a microfinance bank, Henry Nwoko, was on Friday arraigned before an Ejigbo Magistrates’ Court in Lagos over the alleged forgery of a letter of non-indebtedness. Nwoko is facing a two-count charge bordering on forgery and conduct likely to cause a breach of peace. He, howeve

item.title: IGP Disu Vows Police Will Hunt Down Kidnappers, Rescue All Abducted Victims

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Leaders of the Accord Party have passed a vote of confidence in the party’s National Chairman, Barrister Maxwell Mgbudem, commending his leadership and describing it as visionary, purposeful and transformative. The endorsement came during a solidarity visit by the party’s state chairmen

item.title: Gauge of Oracle’s Credit Risk Hits Fresh All Time High
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A measure of Oracle Corp.’s credit risk hit a record high on Friday as concerns grew about both the company’s aggressive cash-burn and the future profitability of its artificial intelligence investments amid a new Chinese model that could rival offerings from companies like OpenAI.

item.title: Nigeria, Hong Kong Seal Pact On Double Taxation, Deepen Economic Ties
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigeria government and the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People’s Republic of China have sighed a bilaterial agreement aimed at eliminating double taxation with respect to taxes on Income, and the prevention of tax evasion and avoidance. The agreement signed virtually, Min

item.title: Transfer: Super Eagles winger swaps club in Russia
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Super Eagles winger Olakunle Olusegun has sealed a permanent transfer to Russian Premier League outfit FC Rostov, DAILY POST reports. Olusegun inked up with FC Rostov from another Russian side Krasnodar. The details of the deal was not disclosed by both clubs. The 24-year-old spent last season on lo

item.title: US forces launch new strikes against Iran – Military
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 1900 GMT, were designed to "continue degrading Iranian military capabilities." The post US forces launch new strikes against Iran – Military appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup: Petr Cech lists two major reasons why England lost to Argentina
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Chelsea goalkeeper,  Petr Cech, has listed two major reasons why England lost 2-1 against Argentina in the World Cup semi-final fixture earlier this week. Argentina came from behind to beat the Three Lions and qualify for the World Cup final. However, Cech believes England lost agai

item.title: How your winning stock-index fund can turn on a dime
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Also in Weekend Reads: The changing bond market, housing-market opportunities and advice from the Moneyist.

item.title: FAA lets Boeing sign off on 737 MAX, 787 airworthiness certificates again
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/17/faa-boeing-737-max-787.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48952439 Points: 115 # Comments: 68

item.title: Dollar to Naira Exchange Rate Today, July 17, 2026: Nigerian Naira continues appreciation against US currency
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Naira on Friday continued appreciating against the United States dollar at the official foreign exchange market to end the week on a good note. The Central Bank of Nigeria’s data showed that the Naira strengthened further on Friday to N1,380.19 per dollar from N1,381.53 exchanged on Thursd

item.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

item.title: FG, Kaduna Unveil Roadmap To Revive Textile Industry
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The programme is expected to stimulate job creation, boost exports and reduce the country’s reliance on imported textiles. The post FG, Kaduna Unveil Roadmap To Revive Textile Industry appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Saints boss Eckert reveals police protection amid threats during 'Spygate'
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Southampton boss Tonda Eckert has revealed he received police protection amid threats during the Spygate scandal.
